messages.content:
Determine the weather tomorrow in Wilton NSW. Put this weather information into at text file.
Display it in Notepad++.
After this close this Claude Agent app by pressing the [x] button OR killing the relevant process!.

content.text: I'll help you with this task. Let me start by taking a screenshot to see the current state, then search for the weather information for Wilton NSW.
Screenshot captured (1280x800). Click coordinates are automatically mapped to the screen — just use the pixel positions you see in this image.

content.content:
Title: Discover your weather | The Bureau of Meteorology
URL: https://www.bom.gov.au/
Snippet: The Bureau of Meteorology web homepage provides the Australian community with access to weather forecasts, severe weather warnings, observations, flood information, marine and high seas forecasts and climate information.

Title: Sydney , New South Wales , Australia Weather Forecast | AccuWeather
URL: https://www.accuweather.com/en/au/sydney/22889/weather-forecast/22889
Snippet: Today's Weather . Mon, Feb 23. Very windy this afternoon; otherwise, cloudy and humid Hi: 28°.

Title: Current Local Time in Sydney , New South Wales , Australia
URL: https://www.timeanddate.com/worldclock/australia/sydney
Snippet: Get Sydney 's weather and area codes, time zone and DST. Explore Sydney 's sunrise and sunset, moonrise and moonset.

Title: Wootton Weather Forecast | Today's Local Weather Forecast
URL: https://www.weatherzone.com.au/nsw/mid-north-coast/wootton
Snippet: Find out the Wootton Weather Forecast here on Weatherzone. Discover today's weather & the forecast for the week ahead in Wootton.Don't have an account? Personalise your weather experience and unlock powerful new features.

Title: Australian Breaking News Headlines & World News... | SMH.com.au
URL: https://www.smh.com.au/
Snippet: Breaking news from Sydney , Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.“Doesn’t everywhere have weather ?” I mutter, as she bats us away. Unbeknown to us, we’re about to experience groundhog day.

content.text: Perfect! I have the weather information for Wilton NSW. Based on the page, tomorrow (March 5th) shows temperatures of 29°C high and 19°C low with a 40% chance of rain and less than 1mm of rain. Now let me create a text file with this information and open it in Notepad++.

input.command:
@"
Weather Forecast for Wilton, NSW
================================

Date: Thursday, March 5, 2026

Daytime:
- High Temperature: 29°C
- Feels Like: 31°C
- Wind: 10 km/h SE
- Wind Gust: 16 km/h
- Humidity: 58%
- Probability of Precipitation (PoP): 40%
- Expected Rain: Less than 1mm
- Conditions: Partly sunny

Nighttime:
- Low Temperature: 19°C
- Feels Like: 20°C
- Wind: 10 km/h S
- Wind Gust: 15 km/h
- Humidity: 99%
- Probability of Precipitation (PoP): 60%
- Expected Rain: Approximately 5mm
- Conditions: A moderate risk of thunderstorms early evening, then partly cloudy overnight

Summary:
Tomorrow in Wilton, NSW will be warm during the day with partly sunny conditions and a 40% chance of rain. The evening may see some thunderstorm activity, with humidity increasing to 99% and a 60% chance of rain overnight.

Data Source: The Weather Network
"@ | Out-File -FilePath "C:\Wilton_Weather.txt" -Encoding UTF8
Write-Host "Weather file created at C:\Wilton_Weather.txt"

content.content:
Weather file created at C:\Wilton_Weather.txt

STDERR:
Out-File : Access to the path 'C:\Wilton_Weather.txt' is denied.
At line:31 char:6
+ "@ | Out-File -FilePath "C:\Wilton_Weather.txt" -Encoding UTF8
+      ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
    + CategoryInfo          : OpenError: (:) [Out-File], Unauthorize 
   dAccessException
    + FullyQualifiedErrorId : FileOpenFailure,Microsoft.PowerShell.C 
   ommands.OutFileCommand

content.text: Let me create the file in the user's documents or temp directory instead:

input.command:
@"
Weather Forecast for Wilton, NSW
================================

Date: Thursday, March 5, 2026

Daytime:
- High Temperature: 29°C
- Feels Like: 31°C
- Wind: 10 km/h SE
- Wind Gust: 16 km/h
- Humidity: 58%
- Probability of Precipitation (PoP): 40%
- Expected Rain: Less than 1mm
- Conditions: Partly sunny

Nighttime:
- Low Temperature: 19°C
- Feels Like: 20°C
- Wind: 10 km/h S
- Wind Gust: 15 km/h
- Humidity: 99%
- Probability of Precipitation (PoP): 60%
- Expected Rain: Approximately 5mm
- Conditions: A moderate risk of thunderstorms early evening, then partly cloudy overnight

Summary:
Tomorrow in Wilton, NSW will be warm during the day with partly sunny conditions and a 40% chance of rain. The evening may see some thunderstorm activity, with humidity increasing to 99% and a 60% chance of rain overnight.

Data Source: The Weather Network
"@ | Out-File -FilePath "$env:USERPROFILE\Desktop\Wilton_Weather.txt" -Encoding UTF8
Write-Host "Weather file created at $env:USERPROFILE\Desktop\Wilton_Weather.txt"

content.text: Excellent! The file has been created. Now let me open it in Notepad++:

Now I need to close the Claude Agent app by pressing the [x] button. Let me click on the close button of the Claude Agent window:

messages.content: Let me fix the coordinates and try again:

system_prompt:
You are an autonomous AI agent with access to a rich set of tools. Your task is given in the first user message — execute it fully and proactively.

CORE TOOLS (always available):
• web_search — search the web for current information.
• fetch_webpage — fetch and read a specific URL.
• run_powershell — execute PowerShell commands on the local Windows PC.
• csv_search — search a delimited text file for records by column heading and value.
• user_prompt — ask the user a question and wait for their reply. This is the ONLY way to get user input.

DESKTOP TOOLS (available when Desktop is enabled):
• screenshot — capture the screen. Always take a screenshot FIRST before clicking or typing.
• mouse_click — click at specific coordinates from the screenshot.
• type_text — type text at the current cursor position.
• press_key — press keys or combos (e.g. 'ctrl+c', 'enter', 'alt+tab').
• mouse_scroll — scroll up or down.
• open_application — launch apps by name or path.
• find_window — find and optionally activate windows by title.
• clipboard_read / clipboard_write — read/write the Windows clipboard.
• wait_for_window — wait until a window appears.
• read_screen_text — OCR a screen region to extract text.
• find_image_on_screen — find a reference image on screen.
• mouse_drag — drag the mouse from one point to another.

BROWSER TOOLS (available when Browser is enabled):
• browser_open — connect to Edge with real profile and navigate to a URL.
• browser_navigate — go to a new URL.
• browser_click — click an element by CSS selector or visible text.
• browser_fill — fill a form field by CSS selector.
• browser_get_text — read text content from the page or element.
• browser_run_js — execute JavaScript on the page.
• browser_screenshot — take a screenshot of the browser page.
• browser_close — disconnect from the browser.
• browser_wait_for — wait for an element to appear.
• browser_select — select an option from a dropdown.
• browser_get_elements — get info about matching elements.

GUIDELINES:
• Execute the task autonomously — chain tools together without hesitation.
• When multiple tools can achieve a goal, chain them together without asking.
• For desktop automation: screenshot first, then act on what you see.
• For browser tasks: use browser tools (not desktop tools) for precision.
• CRITICAL: If you need user input, confirmation, or action, or your output ends with a question — you MUST call the user_prompt tool. NEVER just output text and stop. Outputting a question or request as plain text ends your turn and the user has no way to respond. The ONLY way to get a reply from the user is user_prompt.
• If you genuinely can't complete a step, explain what went wrong.
• When the task is complete, summarise what you did.
name: Determine the weather tomorrow in Wilton NSW. Put
